days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: First impressions in the bustling capital

timeline.title: Arrive at Narita International Airport
timeline.detail: After landing, pass through customs and immigration before proceeding to the JR East rail station for transportation to the city center.

timeline.title: Take Narita Express to Tokyo Station
timeline.detail: Board the Narita Express for a comfortable 1-hour ride to Tokyo Station, enjoying the scenic views along the way.

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
timeline.detail: This hotel in Shinjuku provides modern amenities and is conveniently located within a 5-minute walk from Shinjuku Station.

timeline.title: Explore Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely stroll through this large park featuring traditional Japanese gardens, ideal for relaxing and taking in nature within the city.
timeline.mapQuery: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Dinner at Ichiran Ramen
timeline.detail: Savor an authentic bowl of tonkotsu ramen at this popular chain known for its unique dining experience and flavor-packed broth.

timeline.title: Visit the iconic Shibuya Crossing
timeline.detail: Experience the hustle and bustle as you cross at one of the busiest pedestrian intersections in the world, especially vibrant at night.

localTips: Purchase a Suica or Pasmo card for convenient travel across Tokyo's transit system.
Be mindful of the etiquette at restaurants; use the provided wet towels before eating.
Many restaurants do not accept cash; ensure you have a card handy.
Shinjuku is a vibrant area; explore local bars for a nightcap.
Download the Tokyo subway app for easy navigation.

days.title: Exploring Central Tokyo
days.subtitle: Cultural experiences and shopping

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a hearty breakfast at your hotel, featuring a mix of Western and Japanese options to energize you for the day ahead.

timeline.title: Visit Senso-ji Temple in Asakusa
timeline.detail: Explore Tokyo's oldest temple, Senso-ji, and the surrounding Nakamise shopping street where you can find traditional snacks and souvenirs.

timeline.title: Lunch at Asakusa Menchi
timeline.detail: Try the famous Menchi Katsu sandwich at this popular eatery, made with fresh ground pork and served piping hot.

timeline.title: Explore Ueno Park and Museums
timeline.detail: Visit the sprawling Ueno Park and choose between multiple museums, such as the Tokyo National Museum and the Ueno Zoo.

timeline.title: Shopping in Akihabara
timeline.detail: Dive into the vibrant world of electronics and otaku culture in Akihabara, filled with shops, arcades, and manga cafes.

timeline.title: Dinner at Kizuna Sushi
timeline.detail: Indulge in fresh sushi at this popular spot with a wide variety of offerings, showcasing the best of Japanese seafood.

localTips: Arrive early at Senso-ji to avoid the crowds.
Check museum schedules; many are closed on certain days.
Try unique flavors of KitKat available at Akihabara.
Most shops in Akihabara allow you to test gadgets.
Use Google Translate to assist with menu decisions.

days.title: Day Trip to Nikko
days.subtitle: Nature and heritage

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Fuel up for the day with a breakfast filled with Japanese flavors and pastries before heading for the day trip.

timeline.title: Take Limited Express to Nikko
timeline.detail: Board the Limited Express train at Shinjuku Station, enjoying a scenic ride that lasts about two hours to the stunning town of Nikko.

timeline.title: Visit Toshogu Shrine
timeline.detail: Explore Toshogu Shrine, the ornate final resting place of Tokugawa Ieyasu, surrounded by beautiful nature and intricate carvings.

timeline.title: Lunch at Nikko Yuba Soba
timeline.detail: Enjoy Yuba (tofu skin) soba, a local delicacy, at this top-rated restaurant that highlights the flavors of Nikko.

timeline.title: Explore Lake Chuzenji
timeline.detail: Spend your afternoon by the serene Lake Chuzenji, where you can hike or enjoy breathtaking views of the surrounding mountains.

timeline.title: Return Train to Tokyo
timeline.detail: Take the Limited Express back to Tokyo, allowing you to reflect on the day and relax after your adventure.

timeline.title: Dinner at Gyukatsu Kyoto Katsuguen
timeline.detail: Relish a unique dining experience with gyukatsu, panko-crusted beef served with dipping sauces, at this renowned restaurant.

localTips: Purchase your round-trip train tickets in advance at Shinjuku Station.
Wear comfortable shoes for hiking around Lake Chuzenji.
Check out the free roaming monkeys around the lakeside.
Grab a map from the Nikko tourist information for the best routes.
Be mindful of time; adjust your visits to avoid missing your return train.

days.title: Final Day in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Last-minute explorations

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy your last breakfast in Tokyo, savoring both Western and Japanese options before starting your day.

timeline.title: Stroll through Harajuku
timeline.detail: Explore the quirky fashion scene in Harajuku, particularly along Takeshita Street, known for its vibrant shops and cafes.

timeline.title: Lunch at Kawaii Monster Cafe
timeline.detail: Dine at this colorful and whimsical cafe that embodies Harajuku's youth culture, with bizarre and delightful menu items.

timeline.title: Visit Meiji Shrine
timeline.detail: Immerse yourself in tranquility at this forested shrine. Participate in local rituals and enjoy a peaceful atmosphere just steps from busy Harajuku.

timeline.title: Shopping in Omotesando
timeline.detail: Visit this upscale area known for its designer boutiques and stunning architecture, perfect for last-minute shopping.

timeline.title: Dinner at Narisawa
timeline.detail: Experience one of Tokyo's best dining experiences at this Michelin-starred restaurant, renowned for its innovative Japanese cuisine.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel & Prepare for Departure
timeline.detail: Head back to the hotel for a final stroll around the neighborhood, packing up and reflecting on your memorable trip.

localTips: Try to mix traditional and modern experiences during your last day.
Omotesando becomes beautiful at sunset; plan your visit accordingly.
Visit the local shops for unique souvenirs before leaving Tokyo.
Be mindful of train schedules, as some routes have limited service late at night.
Consider taking a quick stop at a capsule hotel for a unique experience.

hotels.name: Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
hotels.note: 5-min walk from Shinjuku Station, unique Godzilla-themed hotel

budget.note: Return flight from Singapore to Tokyo
Round-trip ticket for Nikko day trip
Pasmo card for transit during the trip
Fees for various activities and attractions
Explore tasty treats with a local guide
Entry to Skytree for breathtaking views

urgent.label: Book Niko Limited Express Train
urgent.note: Seats can fill up fast, book in advance at jrpass.com

urgent.label: Reserve Dinner at Narisawa
urgent.note: Highly popular, reserve at least 1 month in advance.
